---
title: "Absent from Chronic"
subtitle: "AI-Augmented Reproducible Research on Work Absenteeism"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

> *No one beginning a data science project should start from a blinking cursor.*
> — [Megan Risdal](https://towardsdatascience.com/@meganrisdal), Kaggle Product Lead

This project is a **quick-start template for AI-augmented reproducible research**. It combines
reproducible research best practices with an AI support infrastructure that leverages generative
LLMs and agent customization to construct and manage analytic pipelines.

The project applies the template to a real research question: **How many days of work do
employed Canadians miss due to health reasons, and how does that vary across demographic groups?**
Data source: Canadian Community Health Survey (CCHS), cycles 2010–11 and 2013–14.

---

## Site Map

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="48%"}

### Analysis

| Page | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [EDA 1 — Vehicle Performance](analysis/eda-1/eda-1.qmd) | Template example using `mtcars`. Demonstrates data genealogy and graph families. |
| [EDA 2 — Work Absence (CCHS)](analysis/eda-2/eda-2.qmd) | Core analysis. Distribution of `absence_days_total` by sex, age, education, marital status, immigration status, and survey cycle. |

### Data

| Page | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [Data Overview](pages/data.qmd) | Raw inputs, analysis-ready derived datasets, variable definitions, survey design. |
| [Pipeline](pages/pipeline.qmd) | Full Ferry → Ellis execution guide: scripts, outputs, exclusion criteria, troubleshooting. |

:::

::: {.column width="4%"}
:::

::: {.column width="48%"}

### Reference

| Page | Description |
|------|-------------|
| [Guides](pages/guides.qmd) | Getting started, environment management, AI system setup, VS Code tasks. |
| [Philosophy](pages/philosophy.qmd) | Methodology notes: analysis templatization, causal inference, threats to validity, semiology of graphics. |
| [Authors](pages/authors.qmd) | Project team, license, and contribution history. |

:::

::::

---

## AI Persona System

This template includes **9 specialized AI personas**, each optimized for a different research role:

| Persona | Role |
|---------|------|
| Developer | Technical infrastructure and reproducible code |
| Project Manager | Strategic oversight and coordination |
| Research Scientist | Statistical analysis and methodology |
| Prompt Engineer | AI optimization and prompt design |
| Data Engineer | Data pipelines and quality assurance |
| Grapher | Data visualization and exploratory analysis |
| Reporter | Analysis communication and storytelling |
| DevOps Engineer | Deployment and operational excellence |
| Frontend Architect | User interfaces and visualization |

Switch personas in VS Code: `Ctrl+Shift+P` → *Tasks: Run Task* → *Activate [Persona Name] Persona*.

---

## Memory System

The template maintains project continuity across sessions via three files:

- `ai/memory/memory-human.md` — your decisions and reasoning (humans only)
- `ai/memory/memory-ai.md` — AI-maintained technical status (AI only)
- `ai/memory/log/YYYY-MM-DD.md` — per-session logs for isolated change tracking

---

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone https://github.com/oleksandkov/absent-from-chronic-oleksandkov
cd absent-from-chronic-oleksandkov
```

Then open `quick-start-template.Rproj` in RStudio or the folder in VS Code.
Install R dependencies:

```r
Rscript utility/install-packages.R   # flexible: recommended for most users
# or
Rscript utility/init-renv.R          # strict: exact reproducibility
```

See [Guides](pages/guides.qmd) for full setup instructions.

---

*Refer to [RAnalysisSkeleton](https://github.com/wibeasley/RAnalysisSkeleton) for a deeper dive into reproducible research best practices.*
